description: Phase 4: Assess Tier 4 files (596-912 lines) for SRP violations and extract where warranted. Also assess claude-loop.sh status. Most files are expected to be 'no action needed' — this phase is primarily documentation and assessment with one likely extraction (complete.rs).

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
Test count is identical before and after any extraction (run `cargo test 2>&1 | grep 'test result'`)
No breaking changes to existing public APIs — re-export if necessary
Every new module file has a //! module doc comment
EXPLICIT_SKIP decisions documented in tasks/progress.txt with rationale
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Read each file before judging; map responsibilities
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Any extraction compiles and all existing tests pass
3. CORRECTNESS - No orphaned tests, no broken imports, identical test count
4. CODE QUALITY - Module doc comments, pub(crate) visibility, alphabetical mod declarations
5. POLISH - Clean up unused imports in source file after extraction
principles: Assessment is the primary deliverable — document findings clearly
EXPLICIT_SKIP is the expected outcome for most files
Only extract where there is a genuine multi-responsibility smell, not just length
Tests move with their tested functions — never orphan a test

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Run `cargo test 2>&1 | grep 'test result'` and record counts in progress.txt
Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` and confirm zero warnings
Run `cargo fmt --check` and confirm clean

Record baseline line counts for all Tier 4 target files: `wc -l src/commands/complete.rs src/commands/curate/mod.rs src/commands/learnings.rs src/commands/run.rs src/commands/review.rs src/commands/recall.rs src/loop_engine/batch.rs src/error.rs src/commands/irrelevant.rs src/learnings/bandit.rs src/commands/history.rs src/loop_engine/model.rs src/models/learning.rs src/commands/stats.rs src/models/run.rs src/commands/list.rs src/models/task.rs`
Verify Phase 3 modules exist (guidance.rs or documented EXPLICIT_SKIP)
Record line count for scripts/claude-loop.sh
userStories.description: Capture baseline test count after Phase 3 and verify all checks pass.
edgeCases: Phase 3 may have left EXPLICIT_SKIP outcomes — document which extractions were skipped
Some tests may be #[ignore] — count separately
Line counts may differ from PRD estimates if earlier phases touched these files

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Document in progress.txt, proceed only if cargo test passes

invariants: Total test count must not decrease after any extraction
userStories.title: Baseline test count and verify post-Phase-3 state

acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 17 Tier 4 files has a documented assessment in progress.txt
Assessment format per file: file path, line count (prod/test split), responsibilities listed, SRP verdict (COHESIVE / EXTRACT / MONITOR), rationale
Files confirmed as single-responsibility marked 'no action needed' with 1-sentence rationale
Any extraction targets identified with: source file, functions to move, target file, external callers

Assessment specifically addresses: complete.rs (dependency checking + test running mixed with completion), model.rs (50 functions — justified?), error.rs (structural — is 661 lines of error definitions a problem?)
Summary table of all 17 files with verdicts appended to progress.txt
userStories.description: Systematically read each Tier 4 file, document its responsibilities, and determine whether extraction is warranted. For each file: (1) count production vs test lines, (2) list distinct responsibilities, (3) judge SRP compliance, (4) document 'no action needed' or identify specific extraction targets. Expected outcome: most files are cohesive for their scope; complete.rs (912L) and possibly model.rs (622L, 50 functions) are the main candidates.
userStories.notes: This is the core Phase 4 deliverable. P3 learnings to apply: (1) Always check prod vs test line split — oauth.rs was 857L but only ~197L production; high test ratios mean the file is NOT complex, just well-tested. (2) PRD assumptions about file contents can be wrong — read before judging. (3) EXPLICIT_SKIP was the dominant P3 outcome (2/3 files) — expect similar for P4 command files with high test ratios. Exploration suggests: complete.rs has HIGH SRP violations (dependency checking, test running, completion logic, run tracking all mixed). model.rs has 50 functions but may be cohesive (model resolution is a single domain). error.rs is structural (error enum + constructors — expected in Rust). Most command files (learnings, review, recall, history, stats, list) are single-command handlers with high test ratios — likely cohesive. bandit.rs is a pure algorithm. batch.rs is well-scoped. Model files (learning.rs, run.rs, task.rs) are entity definitions with DB parsing. Full P1-P3 extraction scorecard: P1 extracted 5 modules from engine.rs (git_reconcile, output_parsing, prd_reconcile, prompt_sections, worktree), P2 extracted 4 modules (watchdog, status_queries, status_display, calibrate_math) + 1 EXPLICIT_SKIP (detection.rs), P3 extracted 1 module (guidance from signals.rs) + 2 EXPLICIT_SKIPs (oauth.rs, main.rs). EXPLICIT_SKIP rate increased each phase (0/5 → 1/5 → 2/3) as files got smaller. P4 baseline test count: 2264 passed.

correctness: Each file actually read before judging — no assumptions from line count alone
All external callers identified for any extraction candidates
performance: No runtime impact — this is assessment only
style: Assessment documented clearly and concisely in progress.txt
Verdicts are actionable: COHESIVE (no action), EXTRACT (with specific plan), MONITOR (watch for growth)

userStories.title: Assess all Tier 4 files for SRP violations

acceptanceCriteria: Grep results for 'claude-loop' across entire repo documented in progress.txt
Usage verdict: ACTIVE (document scenarios) or DEPRECATED (add comment header)
If deprecated: add comment header to claude-loop.sh: '# DEPRECATED: Superseded by Rust loop engine (src/loop_engine/). Kept as reference implementation.'
If deprecated: note in progress.txt which docs reference it and whether doc updates are needed (but do NOT update docs in this task)
cargo test still passes (script changes don't affect Rust tests)
userStories.description: Determine if scripts/claude-loop.sh is still actively used or has been superseded by the Rust loop engine. Search for references in: README, docs/, CI configs, Makefile, other scripts, src/ code. If superseded: add a deprecation comment header. If still used: document in which scenarios.
userStories.notes: Exploration found: README.md says it 'replaced the external claude-loop.sh script'. docs/INTEGRATION.md has a 'Reference Implementation' section. docs/ARCHITECTURE.md describes it as 'original... 1,455 lines of bash' (now 556 lines). This is very likely DEPRECATED. The agent should verify and mark accordingly.

style: Deprecation comment is clear and explains what replaces it

userStories.title: Assess claude-loop.sh status and deprecate if unused

acceptanceCriteria: Either: identified functions extracted to new module, complete.rs imports them, mod.rs updated, external callers updated, tests moved
Or: EXPLICIT_SKIP documented in progress.txt — assessment found no clean extraction boundary despite multiple concerns
If extracting: new module has //! doc comment
If extracting: grep for all callers of extracted functions and update import paths
cargo test count matches baseline
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.description: If ASSESS-014 confirmed complete.rs has extractable responsibilities, perform the extraction. Likely candidates: dependency validation (get_unsatisfied_deps, check_dependencies_satisfied, are_dependencies_satisfied) → dependency_checker.rs. If assessment says 'no action needed', output EXPLICIT_SKIP.
userStories.notes: This task depends on ASSESS-014's findings. complete.rs (912L) has the highest line count in Tier 4 and exploration found multiple concerns: completion logic, dependency validation, test execution, run tracking. The dependency validation functions (get_unsatisfied_deps, check_dependencies_satisfied, are_dependencies_satisfied) are called from engine.rs and other command files — they form a natural extraction unit. However, the agent should verify this from the ASSESS-014 output before proceeding. P3 learning: check prod/test split first — if complete.rs is mostly tests like oauth.rs was, extraction may not be warranted despite high line count. P3 final note: even when extraction is warranted, the actual extraction (guidance.rs) was ~70 lines of production code + ~130 lines of tests — very small. If complete.rs prod/test split shows <300L production, EXPLICIT_SKIP is likely correct.

correctness: Test count identical before and after
All external callers compile with updated imports
style: Alphabetical mod declaration if new module created

userStories.title: Extract from complete.rs if assessment warrants

Commit: 'chore: LEARN-001 - Update P4 tasks with assessment learnings'
userStories.description: Review assessment findings from ASSESS-014 and ASSESS-015. Update REFACTOR-014 if assessment revealed different extraction targets than expected. Update remaining P4 tasks (reviews, milestones) based on scope of actual changes.
userStories.notes: Assessment learnings applied. ASSESS-014 found 2 EXTRACT targets vs 1 expected (complete.rs + curate/mod.rs). REFACTOR-014 extracted dependency_checker.rs from complete.rs (3 functions, 7 new tests). Two additional extraction candidates identified but deferred: (1) test_validator from complete.rs (~1 function, check_required_tests_pass), (2) dedup from curate/mod.rs (LLM dedup orchestration). Both are low-priority — test_validator is a single function tightly coupled to completion flow, and curate dedup would require careful test separation. Decision: document as MONITOR rather than add REFACTOR-014B/C tasks, since the remaining extractions have marginal SRP benefit and Phase 4 scope is assessment-focused. ASSESS-015 confirmed claude-loop.sh DEPRECATED as expected. Reviews (CODE-REVIEW-1, REFACTOR-REVIEW-1) found no issues — extraction was clean.
userStories.title: Update remaining Phase 4 tasks based on assessment learnings
